Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for vendor rate increase communications using search_emails (query: "rate increase" or "pricing update" or "rate adjustment" or similar). Find two key vendor notices: (1) from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with subject "Pricing Update Effective Immediately -- DOT Documentation Surcharge" announcing a 15% base rate increase plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment on new bookings effective April 11, 2026; and (2) from jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com) with subject "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review" announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026. Both emails are marked is_read: false. The two rate changes have different effective dates, which is critical for the cost impact analysis.
OE 2: Search Slack for vendor rate discussions and David Chen's request to AMs using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "vendor rate" or "Swift" or "Heartland" or "rate increase" or "client exposure" or similar). Find: (1) Alejandro Fuentes's message in #finance (channel C005) about the Q3 vendor-cost exposure model citing both rate notices and flagging that client-by-client margin data does not reconcile across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM; (2) David Chen's message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) asking account owners for client-specific vendor risk assessment -- "Need fast eyes on this from account owners" -- requesting which clients get hit first, whether the issue is price sensitivity or relationship fragility, and whether vendors can be rerouted without damaging trust; (3) Emeka's reply in the same channel noting Mosaic exposure to Heartland cost increases and Swift DOT surcharge complications for moves with specialized handling.
OE 3: Search Airtable relocation records for all 4 clients using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: each company name) or airtable_list_records. Find: Mosaic Robotics -- Andre Washington (Detroit MI to Atlanta GA, Completed), Zara Okoye (Savannah GA to Atlanta GA, Completed, April 14-16, custom crating for robotic arm prototypes), Deshawn Hartley (Tampa FL to Denver CO, In Progress, April 14-19, hard deadline for Automate 2026 trade show), Keiko Tanaka (Columbus OH to Seattle WA, In Progress, April 15-23, Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter Class 3B requiring DOT hazmat placarding), Lena Kowalski (Detroit MI to Salt Lake City UT, In Progress), Andre Mitchell (Columbus OH to Boise ID, In Progress); GreenStack Energy -- Priya Venkatesh (Houston to Seattle, Completed), Noah Fitzgerald (Philadelphia to Seattle, Completed), Tomas Herrera (Chicago to Austin, In Progress), Mei-Lin Zhao (Houston TX to Portland OR, In Progress, April 17-28, Class 9 lithium-ion battery hazmat requiring DOT certification), Joaquin Reyes (Phoenix AZ to Seattle WA, In Progress, April 11-19, 900-lb solar rig overweight transport); Sunbelt Venture Partners -- Marcus Rivera (Miami to Denver, Completed), Jade Thompson (Miami to Denver, Completed), Daniela Cruz (Chicago to Miami, Completed); Palmetto Community Foundation -- Denise Hargrove (Atlanta to Raleigh, Completed). Note that the Airtable Relocations table does not include a Vendor field, so vendor-to-move assignments must be cross-referenced from QuickBooks bills, CRM engagements, emails, and Slack.
OE 4: Search Airtable Client Accounts table using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Account Manager", value: "Emeka Diallo" or by company name). Find: Mosaic Robotics (recAcct000000006, Status: Onboarding, Enterprise Relocation Package), GreenStack Energy (recAcct000000003, Status: Active, Monthly Stipend Fee: $2,000), Sunbelt Venture Partners (recAcct000000066, Status: Active), Palmetto Community Foundation (recAcct000000067, Status: Active). All four accounts are managed by Emeka Diallo.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks invoices for all 4 clients using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by CustomerRef name for each client). Find: Mosaic -- INV-2026-0401 ($4,500, Andre Washington relocation), INV-2026-0306 ($10,000, Lena Kowalski + Andre Mitchell standard relocation packages plus equipment stipend management), INV-2026-0411 ($9,010, 3 April relocations for Zara Okoye, Deshawn Hartley, and Keiko Tanaka, with $2,450 in non-standard surcharges pending client approval), and credit memo CM-2026-0415 (id: cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001, -$90,000 credit for damaged robotic arm prototype Unit #1 from Zara Okoye relocation, insurance claim pending adjudication, $50,000 vendor liability cap, $40,000 gap -- applied against INV-2026-0411); GreenStack -- NMX-2026-0033 ($7,289, Priya Venkatesh apartment/moving/flight), INV-2026-0303 ($5,000, Tomas Herrera standard package), INV-2026-0418 ($9,440, Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes April moves including hazmat and interim storage charges); Sunbelt -- INV-2026-0398 ($4,800), INV-2026-005 ($14,000, original 2 relocations), INV-2026-013 ($7,000, repeat relocation); Palmetto -- INV-2026-0399 ($2,400). The Sunbelt and Palmetto April invoices (INV-2026-0398 and INV-2026-0399) are central to the billing situation the prompt asks about.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks bills for Swift Relocations using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by VendorRef for Swift). Find bills including SR-2026-0421 ($4,450, Priya Venkatesh Houston to Seattle for GreenStack). This maps Swift as the vendor for specific employee moves. Combined with CRM engagement data and email/Slack context, establish that Swift handled: Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack), Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic), and Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack, hazmat). Per the Swift rate notice, the 15% base rate increase applies to all new bookings tendered on or after April 11, and the $500 DOT documentation surcharge applies per shipment where Swift crews manage added compliance paperwork. April moves for Mosaic and GreenStack that were booked before April 11 may still be subject to the DOT surcharge given the compliance documentation requirements on those shipments.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks bills for Heartland Movers and related accruals using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by VendorRef for Heartland, and broader search for DOT/regulatory accruals). Find bills including HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 billed for 8 moves, disputed with $4,800 overbilling -- 3 invalid charges for cancelled or misattributed moves), ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000 contingent liability accrual for Zara Okoye prototype damage), and ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 (id: bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, $50,000 contingent DOT/PHMSA civil penalty accrual for missing placarding certificate on Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment, Mosaic Robotics, Columbus OH to Seattle WA -- estimated per 49 CFR 107.329 minimum single-violation penalty, approved by Marcus Thorne). The ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC bill maps Heartland as the vendor for Zara Okoye's Mosaic move (Savannah to Atlanta). Combined with Linear ticket data (issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack referencing Heartland Movers for overweight permit), Heartland is also identified as the vendor for Joaquin Reyes (GreenStack, Phoenix to Seattle). Heartland's 20% increase is effective July 1 and does not affect April moves.
OE 8: Search CRM for all 4 company records and their associated deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic Robotics", "GreenStack Energy", "Sunbelt Venture Partners", "Palmetto Community Foundation") followed by crm_search_deals (company_id for each, and also by dealname for "Sunbelt" and "Palmetto" since some April billing deals may have null company_id). Find: Mosaic (company_mosaic) -- deal_mosaic_q2 ($4,500, closedwon, Andre Washington Q2 2026 relocation), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 ($2,700, inprogress), deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 ($1,520, inprogress), deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 ($2,770, inprogress), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk (RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM, inprogress); GreenStack (company_greenstack) -- deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes ($9,440, inprogress); Sunbelt (company_sunbelt) -- deal_sunbelt_won ($14,000, closedWon), deal_sunbelt_repeat ($7,000, closedWon), crm_deal_5464b8677e6a ($4,800 April billing, contractsent); Palmetto -- deal_palmetto_won ($3,200, closedWon), crm_deal_7ec892bc6085 ($2,400 April billing, contractsent).
OE 9: List CRM engagements for all 4 companies using crm_list_engagements (company_ids for each). Find: Mosaic has engagement_mosaic_apr2026_relocation_wave (April 2026 relocation wave summary with vendor assignments -- Heartland for Zara Okoye, Swift for Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka, total invoice $9,010) and engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 (DAMAGE INCIDENT -- Prototype Unit #1 valued at $90,000, insurance covers $50,000, $40,000 gap, account at risk). GreenStack has engagement_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes (complex moves summary for Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat Class 9 lithium and Joaquin Reyes 900-lb solar rig). Sunbelt and Palmetto have NO existing CRM engagement notes -- this confirms CRM notes are not current for these two accounts.
OE 10: Search emails for all correspondence with Tamara Byrd at Mosaic Robotics using search_emails (query: "Tamara Byrd" or "tamara.byrd" or "Mosaic" or similar). Find key threads: (1) Damage claim chain -- Tamara's urgent complaint (mosaic_damage_complaint_001, subject "URGENT: Damaged Robotic Arm Prototype -- Zara Okoye Relocation"), Emeka's acknowledgment (mosaic_damage_ack_001), and Emeka's formal incident report (mosaic_incident_report_final_001, subject "Incident Report -- Prototype Damage, Zara Okoye" detailing $90,000 damage to robotic arm Unit #1, $50,000 insurance coverage cap, $40,000 gap, crating specification failure by Heartland Movers crew); (2) Stipend verification request (email_emeka_to_tamara_receipt_verify, subject "Verification request -- Andre Washington's equipment stipend receipt" about the $89 TechComfort ErgoKey Pro keyboard with a byte-for-byte duplicate receipt hash matching another client's submission, reimbursement on hold pending HR confirmation); (3) Mosaic April relocation surcharge reference in Hana Kim's finance email (mosaic_3reloc_hana_finance_001 about INV-2026-0411, $2,450 in non-standard surcharges pending Tamara Byrd approval).
OE 11: Search specifically for any response from Tamara Byrd to the stipend verification request using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "receipt" or "keyboard" or "Andre Washington verification" or similar, looking for replies from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com). Discover that Tamara has NOT replied to the stipend verification email (email_emeka_to_tamara_receipt_verify sent in March 2026). No email from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com exists that references STIP-2026-0094 or the keyboard receipt question. The $89 reimbursement hold remains unresolved. This is the "more sensitive" pending item referenced in the prompt that has been "sitting for a while."
OE 12: Search emails for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "INV-2026-0398" or "INV-2026-0399" or "Sunbelt invoice" or "Palmetto invoice" or similar). Find: (1) Hana Kim's original invoice send emails -- email_email_622650a7a23b to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com for INV-2026-0398 ($4,800) and email_email_9f90308e6a7b to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org for INV-2026-0399 ($2,400), both sent April 10; (2) Marcus Thorne's internal alert (email_email_31433b6d3d67 to david.chen, hana.kim, emeka.diallo, subject "Urgent: Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice mix-up is now pricing exposure + nonprofit documentation issue") explaining that QuickBooks records are correct but the April 10 email send crossed the PDF attachments -- Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit PDF and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial PDF; (3) Client complaints -- Raj's initial question (email_email_348cdfd14d56, is_read: false) and Carmen's complaint (email_email_1f8e937790b9, is_read: false) about the invoice amount not matching the Palmetto quote; (4) Emeka's replies -- email_email_b75ff17bfa19 to Raj and email_email_cf05b9a123a0 to Carmen explaining the attachment error; (5) Unread client follow-ups -- email_email_60e1b29b3e36 from Raj (is_read: false) and email_email_14c1d70d5b74 from Carmen (is_read: false), meaning both clients responded after Emeka's clarification and those responses have not been read yet.
OE 13: Search Linear for existing tickets related to the invoice swap, vendor billing, DOT compliance, stipend issues, and other operational items using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "vendor" or "Heartland" or "DOT" or "stipend" or "Mosaic" or "GreenStack" or similar, with multiple searches as needed). Find: (1) OPS-417 (id: linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130, title: "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation", assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due 2026-04-16, labels: finance, customer-risk, compliance, pricing); (2) issue_heartland_q1_recon (Heartland Movers Q1 vendor invoice reconciliation, state: in_progress); (3) linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response, assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due 2026-04-15); (4) DOT compliance tickets -- issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, in_progress), issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (Mei-Lin Zhao lithium batteries, done), issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (Joaquin Reyes oversize rig / Heartland overweight permit, done), and issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift for Tanaka shipment, urgent priority 1, state: todo); (5) issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (stipend duplicate receipt hash fingerprinting -- references Andre Washington keyboard receipt as one of three flagged transactions, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, priority 1); (6) linear_issue_644c3b2e75a6 (Rebalance April account coverage for Emeka across Sunbelt, Palmetto, Axiom, and Mosaic, assigned to Emeka, priority 2); (7) linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (Invoice email template safety checks to prevent attachment mismatches, in backlog, tied to Sunbelt/Palmetto incident). Read comments on OPS-417 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130") to understand current resolution status.
OE 14: Search Slack for additional context on the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "PDF crossed" or "INV-2026-0398" or similar). Find: (1) Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (channel C005) confirming Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit PDF and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial PDF, flagging this as both revenue-leak risk and nonprofit compliance/documentation risk, with instructions to preserve the April 10 email sends; (2) Emeka's message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) confirming QB records are correct -- Sunbelt INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 and Palmetto INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 -- and that the failure was the April 10 email send where PDF attachments were crossed, with Emeka sending separate correction replies preserving Palmetto's audit trail and clarifying to Sunbelt that this was not a pricing change.
OE 15: Look up contact details for all required email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen", "Tamara Byrd", "Raj Greenfield", "Carmen Delgado-Reyes", "Derek Hollis" or similar). Confirm email addresses: david.chen@moveops.com, tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org, derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com.
OE 16: Send email to David Chen with the vendor rate impact summary (via send_email, sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients: david.chen@moveops.com). The email should contain: (1) a client-by-client breakdown of vendor-to-move mapping across Mosaic, GreenStack, Sunbelt, and Palmetto; (2) the two different rate change structures and effective dates -- Swift 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge effective April 11 vs Heartland 20% effective July 1; (3) cost impact analysis for April moves identifying which moves are affected by the Swift increase (Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka at Mosaic, Mei-Lin Zhao at GreenStack) and which are not yet affected (Heartland moves do not see the increase until Q3); (4) status of the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap and open client follow-ups; (5) the Mosaic damage claim ($90,000 prototype, $40,000 insurance gap); (6) the unresolved Andre Washington stipend verification; and (7) items requiring further ops or finance follow-up.
OE 17: Reply to Raj Greenfield's unread follow-up email about INV-2026-0398 (via reply_to_email, email_id: "email_email_60e1b29b3e36", sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com). The reply should address any remaining questions about the $4,800 invoice amount, confirm that the QuickBooks record is correct for Sunbelt's April services, clarify that the wrong PDF attachment was an administrative error that has been identified and corrected, and offer further assistance or a call if needed.
OE 18: Reply to Carmen Delgado-Reyes's unread follow-up email about INV-2026-0399 (via reply_to_email, email_id: "email_email_14c1d70d5b74", sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com). The reply should address remaining concerns about the $2,400 invoice amount, confirm it reflects the correct nonprofit relocation rate for Palmetto Community Foundation, explain that the wrong PDF was attached due to an administrative error on April 10, and provide a written correction suitable for Palmetto's nonprofit audit trail.
OE 19: Send a follow-up email to Tamara Byrd about the unresolved stipend verification and other open items (via send_email or reply_to_email, sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients: tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com). The email should follow up on the Andre Washington equipment stipend receipt verification (STIP-2026-0094, $89 TechComfort keyboard) that has been pending since March 2026 with no response, and address any other open account-level items such as the $2,450 surcharge approval for the April relocations and the damage claim status.
OE 20: Create one or more Linear tickets for operational follow-up items identified during the investigation (via linear_create_issue, team: "team_operations"). The ticket(s) should cover items needing ops resolution such as: the vendor rate impact assessment for upcoming moves requiring potential vendor reassignment or contract renegotiation, the pending Mosaic surcharge approval ($2,450 on INV-2026-0411), the unresolved stipend hold (STIP-2026-0094), and/or any remaining corrected documentation needed for the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap. Title should reference vendor rate impact or ops follow-up (or similar).
OE 21: Create a CRM engagement note for Mosaic Robotics (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]). The note should document: vendor assignments (Heartland for Zara Okoye, Swift for Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka), vendor rate impact (Swift 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge affects Hartley and Tanaka April moves; Heartland 20% increase not effective until July 1 so Okoye's move is not affected), damage claim status ($90,000 prototype, $50,000 insurance coverage, $40,000 gap, formal incident report filed), unresolved stipend verification (STIP-2026-0094 for Andre Washington, $89, pending since March), surcharge approval pending ($2,450 on INV-2026-0411 awaiting Tamara Byrd sign-off), and current relocation statuses.
OE 22: Create a CRM engagement note for GreenStack Energy (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_greenstack"]). The note should document: vendor assignments (Swift for Priya Venkatesh and Mei-Lin Zhao, Heartland for Joaquin Reyes), vendor rate impact (Swift 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge affects Mei-Lin Zhao's April hazmat shipment; Heartland 20% does not affect Joaquin Reyes until July), current invoice status (NMX-2026-0033 at $7,289, INV-2026-0418 at $9,440), DOT compliance status for Zhao's Class 9 lithium batteries and Reyes' solar rig, and current relocation statuses for all GreenStack employees.
OE 23: Create a CRM engagement note for Sunbelt Venture Partners (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_sunbelt"]). The note should document: invoice swap status (INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 is correct in QuickBooks but the wrong PDF was sent on April 10, OPS-417 filed for corrected documentation), client communication status (Raj Greenfield received initial clarification, unread follow-up from Raj still pending), vendor rate impact assessment (Sunbelt's February relocations used unknown vendor, April relocation for Daniela Cruz vendor not confirmed as Heartland or Swift, limited direct exposure), and that no prior CRM engagement notes existed for this account.
OE 24: Create a CRM engagement note for Palmetto Community Foundation (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]). The note should document: invoice swap status (INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 is correct in QuickBooks, nonprofit rate, but wrong PDF attached on April 10, pricing exposure and audit documentation risk), client communication status (Carmen Delgado-Reyes received initial clarification, unread follow-up from Carmen still pending), that Palmetto has only one completed February relocation (Denise Hargrove, Atlanta to Raleigh) with no April moves, minimal vendor rate impact, and that no prior CRM engagement notes existed for this account.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for vendor rate increase communications using search_emails (query: "rate increase" or "pricing update" or "rate adjustment" or similar). Find two key vendor notices: (1) from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with subject "Pricing Update Effective Immediately -- DOT Documentation Surcharge" announcing a 15% base rate increase plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment on new bookings effective April 11, 2026; and (2) from jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com) with subject "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review" announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026. Both emails are marked is_read: false. The two rate changes have different effective dates, which is critical for the cost impact analysis.
OE 2: Search Slack for vendor rate discussions and David Chen's request to AMs using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "vendor rate" or "Swift" or "Heartland" or "rate increase" or "client exposure" or similar). Find: (1) Alejandro Fuentes's message in #finance (channel C005) about the Q3 vendor-cost exposure model citing both rate notices and flagging that client-by-client margin data does not reconcile across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM; (2) David Chen's message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) asking account owners for client-specific vendor risk assessment -- "Need fast eyes on this from account owners" -- requesting which clients get hit first, whether the issue is price sensitivity or relationship fragility, and whether vendors can be rerouted without damaging trust; (3) Emeka's reply in the same channel noting Mosaic exposure to Heartland cost increases and Swift DOT surcharge complications for moves with specialized handling.
OE 3: Search Airtable relocation records for all 4 clients using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: each company name) or airtable_list_records. Find: Mosaic Robotics -- Andre Washington (Detroit MI to Atlanta GA, Completed), Zara Okoye (Savannah GA to Atlanta GA, Completed, April 14-16, custom crating for robotic arm prototypes), Deshawn Hartley (Tampa FL to Denver CO, In Progress, April 14-19, hard deadline for Automate 2026 trade show), Keiko Tanaka (Columbus OH to Seattle WA, In Progress, April 15-23, Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter Class 3B requiring DOT hazmat placarding), Lena Kowalski (Detroit MI to Salt Lake City UT, In Progress), Andre Mitchell (Columbus OH to Boise ID, In Progress); GreenStack Energy -- Priya Venkatesh (Houston to Seattle, Completed), Noah Fitzgerald (Philadelphia to Seattle, Completed), Tomas Herrera (Chicago to Austin, In Progress), Mei-Lin Zhao (Houston TX to Portland OR, In Progress, April 17-28, Class 9 lithium-ion battery hazmat requiring DOT certification), Joaquin Reyes (Phoenix AZ to Seattle WA, In Progress, April 11-19, 900-lb solar rig overweight transport); Sunbelt Venture Partners -- Marcus Rivera (Miami to Denver, Completed), Jade Thompson (Miami to Denver, Completed), Daniela Cruz (Chicago to Miami, Completed); Palmetto Community Foundation -- Denise Hargrove (Atlanta to Raleigh, Completed). Note that the Airtable Relocations table does not include a Vendor field, so vendor-to-move assignments must be cross-referenced from QuickBooks bills, CRM engagements, emails, and Slack.
OE 4: Search Airtable Client Accounts table using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Account Manager", value: "Emeka Diallo" or by company name). Find: Mosaic Robotics (recAcct000000006, Status: Onboarding, Enterprise Relocation Package), GreenStack Energy (recAcct000000003, Status: Active, Monthly Stipend Fee: $2,000), Sunbelt Venture Partners (recAcct000000066, Status: Active), Palmetto Community Foundation (recAcct000000067, Status: Active). All four accounts are managed by Emeka Diallo.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks invoices for all 4 clients using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by CustomerRef name for each client). Find: Mosaic -- INV-2026-0401 ($4,500, Andre Washington relocation), INV-2026-0306 ($10,000, Lena Kowalski + Andre Mitchell standard relocation packages plus equipment stipend management), INV-2026-0411 ($9,010, 3 April relocations for Zara Okoye, Deshawn Hartley, and Keiko Tanaka, with $2,450 in non-standard surcharges pending client approval), and credit memo CM-2026-0415 (id: cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001, -$90,000 credit for damaged robotic arm prototype Unit #1 from Zara Okoye relocation, insurance claim pending adjudication, $50,000 vendor liability cap, $40,000 gap -- applied against INV-2026-0411); GreenStack -- NMX-2026-0033 ($7,289, Priya Venkatesh apartment/moving/flight), INV-2026-0303 ($5,000, Tomas Herrera standard package), INV-2026-0418 ($9,440, Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes April moves including hazmat and interim storage charges); Sunbelt -- INV-2026-0398 ($4,800), INV-2026-005 ($14,000, original 2 relocations), INV-2026-013 ($7,000, repeat relocation); Palmetto -- INV-2026-0399 ($2,400). The Sunbelt and Palmetto April invoices (INV-2026-0398 and INV-2026-0399) are central to the billing situation the prompt asks about.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks bills for Swift Relocations using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by VendorRef for Swift). Find bills including SR-2026-0421 ($4,450, Priya Venkatesh Houston to Seattle for GreenStack). This maps Swift as the vendor for specific employee moves. Combined with CRM engagement data and email/Slack context, establish that Swift handled: Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack), Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic), and Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack, hazmat). Per the Swift rate notice, the 15% base rate increase applies to all new bookings tendered on or after April 11, and the $500 DOT documentation surcharge applies per shipment where Swift crews manage added compliance paperwork. April moves for Mosaic and GreenStack that were booked before April 11 may still be subject to the DOT surcharge given the compliance documentation requirements on those shipments.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks bills for Heartland Movers and related accruals using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by VendorRef for Heartland, and broader search for DOT/regulatory accruals). Find bills including HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 billed for 8 moves, disputed with $4,800 overbilling -- 3 invalid charges for cancelled or misattributed moves), ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000 contingent liability accrual for Zara Okoye prototype damage), and ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 (id: bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, $50,000 contingent DOT/PHMSA civil penalty accrual for missing placarding certificate on Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment, Mosaic Robotics, Columbus OH to Seattle WA -- estimated per 49 CFR 107.329 minimum single-violation penalty, approved by Marcus Thorne). The ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC bill maps Heartland as the vendor for Zara Okoye's Mosaic move (Savannah to Atlanta). Combined with Linear ticket data (issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack referencing Heartland Movers for overweight permit), Heartland is also identified as the vendor for Joaquin Reyes (GreenStack, Phoenix to Seattle). Heartland's 20% increase is effective July 1 and does not affect April moves.
OE 8: Search CRM for all 4 company records and their associated deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic Robotics", "GreenStack Energy", "Sunbelt Venture Partners", "Palmetto Community Foundation") followed by crm_search_deals (company_id for each, and also by dealname for "Sunbelt" and "Palmetto" since some April billing deals may have null company_id). Find: Mosaic (company_mosaic) -- deal_mosaic_q2 ($4,500, closedwon, Andre Washington Q2 2026 relocation), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 ($2,700, inprogress), deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 ($1,520, inprogress), deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 ($2,770, inprogress), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk (RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM, inprogress); GreenStack (company_greenstack) -- deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes ($9,440, inprogress); Sunbelt (company_sunbelt) -- deal_sunbelt_won ($14,000, closedWon), deal_sunbelt_repeat ($7,000, closedWon), crm_deal_5464b8677e6a ($4,800 April billing, contractsent); Palmetto -- deal_palmetto_won ($3,200, closedWon), crm_deal_7ec892bc6085 ($2,400 April billing, contractsent).
OE 9: List CRM engagements for all 4 companies using crm_list_engagements (company_ids for each). Find: Mosaic has engagement_mosaic_apr2026_relocation_wave (April 2026 relocation wave summary with vendor assignments -- Heartland for Zara Okoye, Swift for Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka, total invoice $9,010) and engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 (DAMAGE INCIDENT -- Prototype Unit #1 valued at $90,000, insurance covers $50,000, $40,000 gap, account at risk). GreenStack has engagement_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes (complex moves summary for Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat Class 9 lithium and Joaquin Reyes 900-lb solar rig). Sunbelt and Palmetto have NO existing CRM engagement notes -- this confirms CRM notes are not current for these two accounts.
OE 10: Search emails for all correspondence with Tamara Byrd at Mosaic Robotics using search_emails (query: "Tamara Byrd" or "tamara.byrd" or "Mosaic" or similar). Find key threads: (1) Damage claim chain -- Tamara's urgent complaint (mosaic_damage_complaint_001, subject "URGENT: Damaged Robotic Arm Prototype -- Zara Okoye Relocation"), Emeka's acknowledgment (mosaic_damage_ack_001), and Emeka's formal incident report (mosaic_incident_report_final_001, subject "Incident Report -- Prototype Damage, Zara Okoye" detailing $90,000 damage to robotic arm Unit #1, $50,000 insurance coverage cap, $40,000 gap, crating specification failure by Heartland Movers crew); (2) Stipend verification request (email_emeka_to_tamara_receipt_verify, subject "Verification request -- Andre Washington's equipment stipend receipt" about the $89 TechComfort ErgoKey Pro keyboard with a byte-for-byte duplicate receipt hash matching another client's submission, reimbursement on hold pending HR confirmation); (3) Mosaic April relocation surcharge reference in Hana Kim's finance email (mosaic_3reloc_hana_finance_001 about INV-2026-0411, $2,450 in non-standard surcharges pending Tamara Byrd approval).
OE 11: Search specifically for any response from Tamara Byrd to the stipend verification request using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "receipt" or "keyboard" or "Andre Washington verification" or similar, looking for replies from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com). Discover that Tamara has NOT replied to the stipend verification email (email_emeka_to_tamara_receipt_verify sent in March 2026). No email from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com exists that references STIP-2026-0094 or the keyboard receipt question. The $89 reimbursement hold remains unresolved. This is the "more sensitive" pending item referenced in the prompt that has been "sitting for a while."
OE 12: Search emails for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "INV-2026-0398" or "INV-2026-0399" or "Sunbelt invoice" or "Palmetto invoice" or similar). Find: (1) Hana Kim's original invoice send emails -- email_email_622650a7a23b to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com for INV-2026-0398 ($4,800) and email_email_9f90308e6a7b to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org for INV-2026-0399 ($2,400), both sent April 10; (2) Marcus Thorne's internal alert (email_email_31433b6d3d67 to david.chen, hana.kim, emeka.diallo, subject "Urgent: Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice mix-up is now pricing exposure + nonprofit documentation issue") explaining that QuickBooks records are correct but the April 10 email send crossed the PDF attachments -- Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit PDF and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial PDF; (3) Client complaints -- Raj's initial question (email_email_348cdfd14d56, is_read: false) and Carmen's complaint (email_email_1f8e937790b9, is_read: false) about the invoice amount not matching the Palmetto quote; (4) Emeka's replies -- email_email_b75ff17bfa19 to Raj and email_email_cf05b9a123a0 to Carmen explaining the attachment error; (5) Unread client follow-ups -- email_email_60e1b29b3e36 from Raj (is_read: false) and email_email_14c1d70d5b74 from Carmen (is_read: false), meaning both clients responded after Emeka's clarification and those responses have not been read yet.
OE 13: Search Linear for existing tickets related to the invoice swap, vendor billing, DOT compliance, stipend issues, and other operational items using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "vendor" or "Heartland" or "DOT" or "stipend" or "Mosaic" or "GreenStack" or similar, with multiple searches as needed). Find: (1) OPS-417 (id: linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130, title: "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation", assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due 2026-04-16, labels: finance, customer-risk, compliance, pricing); (2) issue_heartland_q1_recon (Heartland Movers Q1 vendor invoice reconciliation, state: in_progress); (3) linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response, assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due 2026-04-15); (4) DOT compliance tickets -- issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, in_progress), issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (Mei-Lin Zhao lithium batteries, done), issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (Joaquin Reyes oversize rig / Heartland overweight permit, done), and issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift for Tanaka shipment, urgent priority 1, state: todo); (5) issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (stipend duplicate receipt hash fingerprinting -- references Andre Washington keyboard receipt as one of three flagged transactions, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, priority 1); (6) linear_issue_644c3b2e75a6 (Rebalance April account coverage for Emeka across Sunbelt, Palmetto, Axiom, and Mosaic, assigned to Emeka, priority 2); (7) linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (Invoice email template safety checks to prevent attachment mismatches, in backlog, tied to Sunbelt/Palmetto incident). Read comments on OPS-417 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130") to understand current resolution status.
OE 14: Search Slack for additional context on the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "PDF crossed" or "INV-2026-0398" or similar). Find: (1) Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (channel C005) confirming Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit PDF and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial PDF, flagging this as both revenue-leak risk and nonprofit compliance/documentation risk, with instructions to preserve the April 10 email sends; (2) Emeka's message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) confirming QB records are correct -- Sunbelt INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 and Palmetto INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 -- and that the failure was the April 10 email send where PDF attachments were crossed, with Emeka sending separate correction replies preserving Palmetto's audit trail and clarifying to Sunbelt that this was not a pricing change.
OE 15: Look up contact details for all required email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen", "Tamara Byrd", "Raj Greenfield", "Carmen Delgado-Reyes", "Derek Hollis" or similar). Confirm email addresses: david.chen@moveops.com, tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org, derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com.
OE 16: Send email to David Chen with the vendor rate impact summary (via send_email, sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients: david.chen@moveops.com). The email should contain: (1) a client-by-client breakdown of vendor-to-move mapping across Mosaic, GreenStack, Sunbelt, and Palmetto; (2) the two different rate change structures and effective dates -- Swift 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge effective April 11 vs Heartland 20% effective July 1; (3) cost impact analysis for April moves identifying which moves are affected by the Swift increase (Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka at Mosaic, Mei-Lin Zhao at GreenStack) and which are not yet affected (Heartland moves do not see the increase until Q3); (4) status of the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap and open client follow-ups; (5) the Mosaic damage claim ($90,000 prototype, $40,000 insurance gap); (6) the unresolved Andre Washington stipend verification; and (7) items requiring further ops or finance follow-up.
OE 17: Reply to Raj Greenfield's unread follow-up email about INV-2026-0398 (via reply_to_email, email_id: "email_email_60e1b29b3e36", sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com). The reply should address any remaining questions about the $4,800 invoice amount, confirm that the QuickBooks record is correct for Sunbelt's April services, clarify that the wrong PDF attachment was an administrative error that has been identified and corrected, and offer further assistance or a call if needed.
OE 18: Reply to Carmen Delgado-Reyes's unread follow-up email about INV-2026-0399 (via reply_to_email, email_id: "email_email_14c1d70d5b74", sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com). The reply should address remaining concerns about the $2,400 invoice amount, confirm it reflects the correct nonprofit relocation rate for Palmetto Community Foundation, explain that the wrong PDF was attached due to an administrative error on April 10, and provide a written correction suitable for Palmetto's nonprofit audit trail.
OE 19: Send a follow-up email to Tamara Byrd about the unresolved stipend verification and other open items (via send_email or reply_to_email, sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients: tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com). The email should follow up on the Andre Washington equipment stipend receipt verification (STIP-2026-0094, $89 TechComfort keyboard) that has been pending since March 2026 with no response, and address any other open account-level items such as the $2,450 surcharge approval for the April relocations and the damage claim status.
OE 20: Create one or more Linear tickets for operational follow-up items identified during the investigation (via linear_create_issue, team: "team_operations"). The ticket(s) should cover items needing ops resolution such as: the vendor rate impact assessment for upcoming moves requiring potential vendor reassignment or contract renegotiation, the pending Mosaic surcharge approval ($2,450 on INV-2026-0411), the unresolved stipend hold (STIP-2026-0094), and/or any remaining corrected documentation needed for the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap. Title should reference vendor rate impact or ops follow-up (or similar).
OE 21: Create a CRM engagement note for Mosaic Robotics (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]). The note should document: vendor assignments (Heartland for Zara Okoye, Swift for Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka), vendor rate impact (Swift 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge affects Hartley and Tanaka April moves; Heartland 20% increase not effective until July 1 so Okoye's move is not affected), damage claim status ($90,000 prototype, $50,000 insurance coverage, $40,000 gap, formal incident report filed), unresolved stipend verification (STIP-2026-0094 for Andre Washington, $89, pending since March), surcharge approval pending ($2,450 on INV-2026-0411 awaiting Tamara Byrd sign-off), and current relocation statuses.
OE 22: Create a CRM engagement note for GreenStack Energy (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_greenstack"]). The note should document: vendor assignments (Swift for Priya Venkatesh and Mei-Lin Zhao, Heartland for Joaquin Reyes), vendor rate impact (Swift 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge affects Mei-Lin Zhao's April hazmat shipment; Heartland 20% does not affect Joaquin Reyes until July), current invoice status (NMX-2026-0033 at $7,289, INV-2026-0418 at $9,440), DOT compliance status for Zhao's Class 9 lithium batteries and Reyes' solar rig, and current relocation statuses for all GreenStack employees.
OE 23: Create a CRM engagement note for Sunbelt Venture Partners (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_sunbelt"]). The note should document: invoice swap status (INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 is correct in QuickBooks but the wrong PDF was sent on April 10, OPS-417 filed for corrected documentation), client communication status (Raj Greenfield received initial clarification, unread follow-up from Raj still pending), vendor rate impact assessment (Sunbelt's February relocations used unknown vendor, April relocation for Daniela Cruz vendor not confirmed as Heartland or Swift, limited direct exposure), and that no prior CRM engagement notes existed for this account.
OE 24: Create a CRM engagement note for Palmetto Community Foundation (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]). The note should document: invoice swap status (INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 is correct in QuickBooks, nonprofit rate, but wrong PDF attached on April 10, pricing exposure and audit documentation risk), client communication status (Carmen Delgado-Reyes received initial clarification, unread follow-up from Carmen still pending), that Palmetto has only one completed February relocation (Denise Hargrove, Atlanta to Raleigh) with no April moves, minimal vendor rate impact, and that no prior CRM engagement notes existed for this account.

18 The final response identifies that the Sunbelt/Palmetto billing issue is a PDF attachment swap from the April 10 email send, where Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit PDF and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial PDF, not a QuickBooks data error (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response states 'Invoice PDFs were swapped on April 10. QuickBooks was correct but each client got the other's document.' This correctly identifies the root cause as a PDF attachment swap rather than a QuickBooks data error.
✓Run #2The final response states the billing issue was confirmed as a 'PDF swap — not clean'. The CRM note for Sunbelt explicitly explains: 'April 10 batch email send by Hana Kim attached wrong invoice PDFs: Sunbelt received Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit invoice PDF, Palmetto received Sunbelt's $4,800 commercial PDF. QuickBooks records are correct.' This correctly identifies the root cause as an email attachment swap, not a QuickBooks data error.
✓Run #3The final response states the billing situation is 'NOT clean' and correctly identifies the root cause as 'Hana's invoice batch swapped the PDF attachments.' The existing Linear ticket OPS-417 (found by the agent) also confirms 'QuickBooks invoices are correct, but the emailed PDFs sent on 2026-04-10 were swapped.' The agent correctly identifies this as an attachment/email send error, not a QuickBooks data error.
✓Run #4The final response states 'Billing is NOT clean' for both Sunbelt and Palmetto, and the email to David explicitly states 'What failed was the outbound email attachment layer. The PDFs sent on April 10 do not match the QuickBooks records' — identifying the root cause as a PDF attachment swap error, not a QuickBooks data error.
✓Run #5The final response clearly states: 'QuickBooks is correct (INV-2026-0398 = $4,800 Sunbelt, INV-2026-0399 = $2,400 Palmetto), but the client-facing PDFs were swapped on April 10.' This correctly identifies the root cause as a PDF attachment swap (email send error), not a QuickBooks data error.
✓Run #6The final response and the David email both identify the root cause as crossed PDF attachments sent on April 10: 'Invoices INV-2026-0398 (Sunbelt, $4,800) and INV-2026-0399 (Palmetto, $2,400) were sent with SWAPPED PDF attachments on April 10.' This is correctly identified as a batch email send error, not a QuickBooks data error.

outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✗ 67%

I sent Tamara a verification request on March 13 and have not received a response.' The email to Tamara also confirms this was unresponded to.
✗Run #3The final response states 'Tamara has NOT responded to any of your recent communications. Her last inbound was the damage complaint on April 15.' This is a general statement about all communications, not specifically identifying that Tamara failed to respond to the Andre Washington stipend receipt verification email sent in March. The stipend fraud is mentioned as a separate issue but the connection to Tamara's non-response on that specific thread is not made.
✓Run #4The final response states Tamara 'has not responded to any of them' including 'the stipend fraud verification ($89)'. The email to Tamara also confirms this was an outstanding March 13 request with no response received.
✓Run #5The final response states: 'Tamara had NOT responded to any of these individually — they've all been sitting.' This explicitly identifies that Tamara did not respond to any of the pending items, including the stipend receipt verification from March.
✗Run #6The final response does not mention the Andre Washington stipend receipt verification email or Tamara's non-response to it. The agent searched for the stipend issue but found no relevant email. The final response only lists two pending Tamara items (damage incident report and surcharge approval), omitting any reference to the unanswered stipend verification from March.
20 The final response identifies the Mosaic prototype damage claim as involving $90,000 in damages with $50,000 insurance coverage and a $40,000 gap (or similar financial details).
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✗✗✓ 67%
✓Run #1The final response mentions 'Mosaic's $90K damage claim ($40K gap)' and the email to David specifies '$90K prototype damage claim' with 'vendor liability caps at $50K/item, leaving a $40K uncovered gap' — all three figures present.
✓Run #2The email to David Chen explicitly states: 'The $40K insurance gap (our policy covers $50K, prototype valued at $90K) remains unresolved.' The '(a) $90K Prototype Damage Claim' section clearly details the $90,000 damage, $50,000 insurance cap, and $40,000 gap.
✓Run #3The final response clearly states '$90K prototype damage claim' with '$40K exposure gap above our $50K vendor coverage cap,' matching the required financial details of $90,000 total, $50,000 insurance cap, and $40,000 uninsured gap.
✗Run #4The final response states 'we have $90K in damage liability + $50K DOT penalty accrual' — this conflates the $50K DOT penalty with the $50K insurance cap. The $40K uninsured gap is not mentioned in the final response text. While the email to David correctly states '$90K damage vs. $50K vendor liability cap' and '$40K direct exposure', the final response to the user does not include these specific financial details.
✗Run #5The final response text does not mention $90,000 in damages, $50,000 insurance coverage, or a $40,000 gap. While the email to David contained these figures, the final summary response only mentions 'the damage incident report status' without the specific financial details required by this criterion.
✓Run #6The final response states 'The damage claim also has a $40K direct MoveOps exposure gap above our $50K vendor liability coverage.' The David email also explicitly states: '$90K contingent liability accrued... MoveOps vendor liability coverage caps at $50K; remaining $40K is our direct exposure.' All three figures ($90K, $50K, $40K) are correctly identified.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used crm_search_companies (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Sunbelt, Palmetto), crm_search_deals (for multiple companies), and crm_list_engagements to look up client account data.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows multiple crm_search_companies calls (Mosaic, GreenStack, Sunbelt, Palmetto), crm_search_deals calls for each company, and a crm_list_engagements call for company_mosaic.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows crm_search_companies called four times with name parameters 'Mosaic', 'GreenStack', 'Sunbelt', and 'Palmetto' to look up all four client accounts before performing any CRM updates.
✓Run #4The trajectory includes multiple crm_search_companies calls (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Sunbelt, Palmetto), crm_search_deals calls, and crm_list_engagements calls to look up client account data.
✓Run #5The trajectory contains multiple crm_search_companies calls (for Mosaic, GreenStack, Sunbelt, Palmetto), crm_search_deals calls, and crm_list_engagements calls to look up client account data.
✓Run #6The agent used crm_search_companies to search for all four clients (Mosaic, GreenStack, Sunbelt, Palmetto) at the start of the trajectory to look up client account data.
24 The model must use a Linear read tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) to search for existing operational tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1A linear_list_issues call was made with input {'assignee': 'Emeka'} to search for existing operational tickets before creating new ones.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows two linear_list_issues calls: one with query 'Mosaic' and one with query 'Sunbelt Palmetto invoice', before creating the new Linear ticket.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows linear_list_issues called with parameters {team: 'Operations', limit: 30} to retrieve existing operational tickets before creating new ones. This correctly checked for existing tickets like OPS-417.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows two linear_list_issues calls (with queries 'Mosaic' and 'vendor rate') before creating new tickets, demonstrating the agent searched for existing issues first.
✓Run #5The trajectory contains a linear_list_issues call with limit: 50, which retrieves existing Linear issues before creating new tickets.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries for 'Mosaic', 'Sunbelt', 'Palmetto', 'GreenStack', 'vendor rate', and 'invoice swap' to search for existing operational tickets before creating new ones.
25 The model must pass a query related to "invoice," "Sunbelt," "Palmetto," "vendor," "DOT," or "Mosaic" (or similar) when calling linear_list_issues to find existing operational tickets.
query construction
 ✗✓✗✓✗✓ 50%
✗Run #1The linear_list_issues call used only {'assignee': 'Emeka'} as the parameter. No query referencing 'invoice,' 'Sunbelt,' 'Palmetto,' 'vendor,' 'DOT,' or 'Mosaic' was passed. This means the agent could miss existing tickets on those topics (like OPS-417) that are not assigned to Emeka.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows linear_list_issues was called with query 'Mosaic' and again with query 'Sunbelt Palmetto invoice', both of which match the criterion's required terms related to invoice, Sunbelt, Palmetto, and Mosaic.
✗Run #3The linear_list_issues call used parameters {team: 'Operations', limit: 30} with no query/search parameter related to 'invoice', 'Sunbelt', 'Palmetto', 'vendor', 'DOT', or 'Mosaic'. The agent relied on fetching all Operations team issues by limit rather than using a targeted query to find relevant existing tickets.
✓Run #4The linear_list_issues calls used queries 'Mosaic' (matching the Mosaic criterion) and 'vendor rate' (matching the vendor criterion). Both queries are directly relevant to finding existing operational tickets related to the accounts and issues in question.
✗Run #5The linear_list_issues call was made with only {'limit': 50} — no query parameter referencing 'invoice', 'Sunbelt', 'Palmetto', 'vendor', 'DOT', 'Mosaic', or any other relevant terms. The agent retrieved all issues without filtering, meaning it could miss existing tickets with poor signal if the full list was long, and did not demonstrate targeted query construction.
✓Run #6The agent passed queries including 'Mosaic', 'Sunbelt', 'Palmetto', 'GreenStack', 'vendor rate', and 'invoice swap' to linear_list_issues — all directly relevant to invoices, clients, vendor billing, and Mosaic/Sunbelt/Palmetto topics as specified in the criterion.
Total 22/2523/2520/2523/2521/2523/25 88%
